name: Awakening Flow
description: Pre-purchase nurture from presence-quiz completion to first order. Hope always leads; the gap stays grey subtext. Never sells during Seen.

touchpoints.title: Your presence score
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Deliver quiz results instantly — the first proof someone noticed. An archetype and a reclaimable number, never a grade.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Tone: calm, second person, present tense. Hope leads; the gap is grey subtext. One written 10-second pause. No product pitch — this is Seen, and we never sell during Seen.

touchpoints.title: The moment you noticed
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Mirror the reader's autopilot day — Maya's Tuesday — so they feel named, not sold to.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Concrete over cosmic: kitchen counters, cold toast, the school run. Invite, never instruct. One written 10-second pause in the body. No product mention, no urgency.

touchpoints.title: What autopilot costs
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Problem education: attention residue and days that blur. Builds the cost of the gap without naming the product.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Present tense, one breath per sentence. No product mention, no statistics theater, no urgency. Close on the written 10-second pause.

touchpoints.title: A pulse instead of a ping
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Explain the mechanism: a gentle physical pulse a few times an hour — no screen, no notification, nothing to check.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Quiet mechanism reveal. Never reference tracking, data, or scores — the ring reports nothing, and that is the point. 'You may notice', never 'you will feel'. One pause, one soft link.

touchpoints.title: One reader's Tuesday, after
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Social proof story matched to the reader's quiz archetype — one ordinary Tuesday, quietly changed.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One concrete story, no superlatives, no exclamation points. Ban journey/transform/unlock/elevate. Include the written 10-second pause.

touchpoints.title: Try this before you buy anything
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Give a free 60-second presence practice — generosity before commerce.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The practice is the whole email; the written 10-second pause is the product here. Invite, never instruct. Single soft link to the Presence Experience, no buy CTA.

touchpoints.title: Your questions, answered
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Answer the practical objections plainly: sizing, battery, 'another device?', returns, water.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Plain answers, one per breath. Reinforce: no tracking, no data, nothing to check. Invite replies to care@ — never no-reply. One written pause before the sign-off.

touchpoints.title: The Presence Experience
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Invite the reader into the longform immersion page — feel the practice before owning the object.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Short letter, one lime CTA to the Presence Experience page. Present tense, second person. Written 10-second pause before the CTA so the click is a choice, not a reflex.

touchpoints.title: When would now be a good time?
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Soft close: guarantee, free size exchange, a single calm CTA to the product page. Buy CTAs are allowed — this is Convinced.
touchpoints.contentBrief: No scarcity, no countdown, no urgency. One lime CTA to index.html. State the guarantee and free size exchange in one breath each. Written 10-second pause before the CTA.

touchpoints.title: We'll be here
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Downshift to a monthly rhythm with an explicit opt-down. Respecting attention IS the brand.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Offer fewer emails as a gift, not a loss. No win-back tricks, no urgency. One written pause; a single quiet link at most.

touchpoints.title: Still thinking it over
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Cart-abandon recovery: restate the guarantee and the sizing safety net with one calm CTA.
touchpoints.contentBrief: No urgency, no discount pressure, no countdown. Name the two real hesitations (fit, commitment) and answer both. One lime CTA to index.html; one written 10-second pause.
touchpoints.triggerCondition: Cart abandoned on index.html; no purchase within 24h

name: True Fit Flow
description: Order confirmation through the sizing fork to confirmed fit, with a free-exchange safety net. Sizing is the #1 DTC ring failure mode.

touchpoints.title: Your ring is coming
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Order confirmation that lands as a beginning: the decision itself was the first act of presence.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Transactional sender ('Pulse' hello@, Reply-To care@). Dark luxe world — the ring is the hero. Calm facts first (order, timing), then one written 10-second pause. No upsells.

touchpoints.title: First, the right fit
touchpoints.strategicIntent: The sizing fork: confirmed size ships now; unsure gets a sizing kit. One question, nothing else.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One question, two buttons — 'I know my size' / 'Send me a sizer'. Make choosing effortless; no other content, no cross-sell. Calm, second person, present tense.
touchpoints.triggerCondition: Ring size not confirmed at checkout

touchpoints.title: Your sizing kit is here
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Teach the measure: wear the sizer overnight, check the knuckle, size the dominant hand.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Three short steps, one per breath. Invite, never drill-sergeant. No deadline pressure — but confirming is one tap.
touchpoints.knownRisks: If the sizer is not worn overnight, sizes run small and exchange volume rises.
touchpoints.triggerCondition: Chose 'send me a sizer' at the fork

touchpoints.title: Confirm your size
touchpoints.strategicIntent: One-tap size confirmation; one record per customer.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Single lime CTA — the confirmation tap. Restate the free-exchange safety net in one line so the tap feels low-stakes. No urgency.

touchpoints.title: A gift of presence
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Gift-giver confirmation: capture the gift note and offer a discreet recipient sizing link.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Speak to the giver's intention — presence, given away. The gift note is the one ask. Dark luxe world. The recipient path never reveals pricing.

touchpoints.title: Exchange, made simple
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Free size exchange executed without friction — kills the #1 return reason.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Zero apology theater, zero blame. Three calm steps and a prepaid label. One breath per sentence; one written pause while the new size ships.

touchpoints.title: Delivery updates, by text
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Clear, explicit SMS opt-in at checkout — the one place we ask for a phone number. Consent is the product behaving like itself.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Phone field + two separate checkboxes (delivery updates / weekly pause texts). Full consent language: message frequency, msg&data rates, STOP to opt out, HELP for help, privacy link. Never pre-checked.
touchpoints.knownRisks: Implied consent is not consent. Without explicit opt-in language we cannot text at all.

touchpoints.title: Confirmed — you're on the list
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Double opt-in confirmation: restate what they signed up for, how often, and how to leave.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One message: what we'll text, a-few-a-month frequency, STOP/HELP line. Nothing else.
touchpoints.triggerCondition: Phone number submitted with opt-in checked

name: Arrival Flow
description: Ship notice through unboxing, charging, app pairing, and the first pulse. The ceremonial handoff between the product world and the human world.

touchpoints.title: It's on its way
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Ceremonial ship notice plus permission to slow down while it travels.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Dark luxe world — the ring nested in its box. Tracking link once, no delivery anxiety. One written 10-second pause: the practice starts before the ring does.

touchpoints.title: Before it arrives
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Prepare the arrival: download the app, choose a charging spot you will see nightly.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Two small tasks, invited not assigned. Frame the charging spot as a place in the home, not a device chore. Cream world; one written pause.

touchpoints.title: It's here
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Delivery confirmation with one instruction: open it when you have five quiet minutes.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One or two lines. No setup pressure on delivery day. The pause here is literal — wait for five quiet minutes before opening.

touchpoints.title: Charge, pair, breathe
touchpoints.strategicIntent: The unbox ritual: full charge, app pairing, one breath before first wear.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The deliberate crossover email: cream letter template with a product-in-life hero — the temperature change between worlds. Three steps; the third is a breath. Pairing sets pulse windows — setup, never tracking.

touchpoints.title: Your first pulse
touchpoints.strategicIntent: A guided 90-second first-pulse arrival ritual — the threshold into practice.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One screen, one voice, 90 seconds. Second person, present tense. No account creation, no data capture — arrive, feel one pulse, done.

touchpoints.title: Someone thought of you
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Gift-recipient welcome: who sent it and why (opt-in note), with their own setup path. They start at Seen, not Relieved.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Lead with the giver's note if opted in. Assume nothing — earn the wrist. A gentle setup path at their own pace; one written 10-second pause.

name: First 30 Days Flow
description: New owner builds the wear-charge-notice habit over 30 days, ending with a felt presence shift measured only by the re-taken quiz.

touchpoints.title: Day one: just wear it
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Zero-goal start: the only job today is the ring on your finger.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One idea only. Explicitly no goals, no setup homework. Success is the email open — never wear detection; the ring reports nothing. One written 10-second pause.

touchpoints.title: Your pulse windows
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Set three daily pulse windows anchored to real moments — commute, lunch, bedtime. Setup, not tracking.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Frame windows as appointments with yourself, anchored to moments that already exist. App push is fine here — it is a setup task, not tracking. Nothing is measured, nothing is reported.

touchpoints.title: What did you notice?
touchpoints.strategicIntent: First reflection: one word for before the pulse, one word for after.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The ask is two words in a reply. Prompt box with the honey left-border. No right answers — 'nothing yet' is a fine answer. Written pause before the prompt.

touchpoints.title: No rush
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Not-yet-paired variant: an unhurried founder note that removes all pressure to begin.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Founder voice, three short paragraphs. No guilt, no 'we noticed you haven't' — the trigger is engagement-based, never telemetry. The ring will keep.

touchpoints.title: How is it going with your ring?
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Active variant: one 1-10 feeling rating plus an open comment ask.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One tap plus one optional sentence. The rating is how it feels — never usage data. Thank them either way in the confirmation.

touchpoints.title: Charge while you shower
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Anchor charging to an existing daily ritual — a dead ring is a dead practice.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One line, one anchor habit. No streak framing, no nagging cadence. Calm, second person, present tense.
touchpoints.successCriteria: Not measured directly — supports the charge habit and Day-30 completion

touchpoints.title: Week one: what to notice
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Somatic education: the pulse works before you consciously register it.
touchpoints.contentBrief: 'You may notice', never 'you will feel'. Concrete bodily anchors — shoulders, jaw, breath. One written 10-second pause mid-email.

touchpoints.title: The pause is working
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Reflection without measurement: count nothing; recall one moment this week you returned.
touchpoints.contentBrief: NO app-data playback of any kind — no counts, no charts, no 'you paused N times'. The evidence is a remembered moment; ask for it in a reply. One written pause.
touchpoints.knownRisks: Any hint of usage playback here breaks the anti-data promise at its most tempting moment.

touchpoints.title: Two weeks of returning
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Time-based recognition — two weeks since pairing — plus a pulse-window tune-up prompt.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Recognition is calendar-based, never a streak count. Invite one adjustment to pulse windows now that real life has weighed in. One breath per sentence.

touchpoints.title: The 21-day threshold
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Habit science: what repetition does in the nervous system at three weeks.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One research-grounded idea in plain language — no citation theater. Present tense, second person, concrete. End on the written 10-second pause.

touchpoints.title: Thirty days present
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Milestone plus quiz re-take invite: measure the shift they can already feel.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The re-take is the only measurement this brand does — self-reported, re-takeable, theirs. One lime CTA to the quiz. Never a grade, never telemetry.

touchpoints.title: Your presence report
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Before/after quiz delta rendered as an archetype shift and reclaimed-life framing. Graduates the reader to owner.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Self-reported delta only — never telemetry, never a grade. Hope leads; the remaining gap is grey subtext. The shareable card is opt-in.
touchpoints.knownRisks: If the delta reads as a score or grade, it betrays the anti-data promise on the most-viewed page of the arc.

name: The Sunday Pause
description: Recurring Sunday-evening theme release plus dormancy win-back. The weekly ritual that keeps the ring out of the drawer.

touchpoints.title: The Sunday Pause — Arrive
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Weekly Sunday-evening theme release with a matching pulse pattern in the app.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Subject pattern 'The Sunday Pause — [theme]'. One theme, one paragraph, one pattern to activate. The written 10-second pause is the theme's first practice.

touchpoints.title: Midweek return
touchpoints.strategicIntent: One line midweek: the theme lives in the body, not the inbox.
touchpoints.contentBrief: A single sentence tied to this week's theme. No link required. No streaks, no check-ins, no urgency.
touchpoints.successCriteria: Not measured directly — sustains the weekly theme between Sundays

touchpoints.title: What others noticed this week
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Community reflections as quiet social proof — no leaderboard, no rankings.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Three short reader reflections, first names only, always opt-in. No metrics, no 'top' anything. One written pause before the sign-off.

touchpoints.title: Come back to now
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Win-back without guilt: one small step — charge it tonight, wear it tomorrow.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Trigger is engagement-based (21 days of zero email/app engagement) — never wear data. No guilt, no 'we miss you' theater. Two small steps, and the quiz re-take as a re-entry door.
touchpoints.knownRisks: Any guilt tone confirms the drawer decision instead of reversing it.

touchpoints.title: One minute, no ring required
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Re-enter through practice, not product: a ringless 60-second presence practice, then 30 days of quiet.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The practice asks nothing of the ring or the app. State the coming quiet explicitly — 'we'll write once a month unless you say otherwise'. Generosity is the last word.

touchpoints.title: The Pause, by text
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Invite owners who never gave a number to opt in to the midweek text — the quietest channel for the quietest nudge.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One idea: a single line on Wednesdays, no links most weeks. Opt-in page linked; consent language on the page, not implied here.

name: Ripple Flow
description: Reviews, referrals, gifting, and shareable presence stories — love becomes the referral, starting from the Day-30 glow.

touchpoints.title: Be Here WOW
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Review ask while the Day-30 glow is fresh.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Ask for words, not stars — the rating is the afterthought. One lime CTA. No incentives; the story is the gift. One written pause before the ask.

touchpoints.title: Your story matters
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Testimonial ask that feeds the Friday community email and on-site proof.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Explain exactly where their words go — Friday email, site — first names only, consent explicit. One reply prompt, no urgency.

touchpoints.title: Give a month of presence
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Referral link framed as a gift rather than a commission.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Gift framing only — no 'earn $20' language. One lime CTA. Written 10-second pause before the ask: who came to mind just now?

touchpoints.title: Someone you love is on autopilot too
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Direct gift prompt — gift the exact person they named in the quiz, so love becomes the referral.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Name the quiz moment back to them: 'you told us who you think of'. One CTA to gift. No pressure; the written pause is for picturing that person's Tuesday.

touchpoints.title: What you've given yourself
touchpoints.strategicIntent: A shareable presence story card built from their own self-reported shift.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The card is built from quiz self-reports only — never telemetry. Sharing is one tap, entirely optional, and beautiful enough to want to.

touchpoints.title: Join the Presence Circle
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Community invite, earned at six weeks.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Frame the Circle as people, not a channel. What happens there, one breath each. One lime CTA to join; no FOMO framing.

touchpoints.title: Your story kit
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Assets and language so they can tell their story their own way.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Equip, don't script: prompts, images, and starting lines they can freely discard. Copy bans hold: no journey/transform/unlock/elevate.

name: Inner Circle Flow
description: Manually enrolled advocates: recognition, story capture, and co-creation. The First Hundred.

touchpoints.title: We noticed your practice
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Founder recognition of their practice and generosity — never their purchase volume.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Personally written, personally signed (Johan). Reference something true and specific they shared. 'We noticed' means their words and replies — never usage data.

touchpoints.title: Here's what you can share
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Share kit: language, prompts, and assets. Equip, don't script.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Everything optional, everything editable. No talking points, no hashtag mandates. One written pause: what would you actually say to a friend?

touchpoints.title: Can we tell your story?
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Personal founder ask for a filmed or longform story.
touchpoints.contentBrief: The ask is personal and revocable at any point. The email opens the door; the call (script attached) does the asking. Meaning over money — no compensation framing.

touchpoints.title: You're showing others the way
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Amplify what they shared — celebration, not extraction.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Show exactly where their words landed and what a reader said back. Gratitude with no follow-up ask attached.

touchpoints.title: The First Hundred
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Name the early-advocate circle: early pattern access and a direct founder line.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Named, small, and real: The First Hundred. Two privileges only — early pulse patterns, a direct line to Johan. Accepting is one reply.

touchpoints.title: Help shape what's next
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Co-creation: beta pulse patterns, app input, named in release notes.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Specific asks with real influence — a pattern to test, one question to answer. Credit by name in release notes, always with permission.

name: Spaces Flow
description: Studios, spas, and corporate wellness — from inquiry to launch to an aggregate team presence report built only on self-reported quiz deltas.

touchpoints.title: We received your inquiry
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Warm acknowledgment that routes studio/spa vs corporate from the first touch.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Reply within the business day, signed by a person. One routing question, plainly asked. Calm B2B register — still no urgency, still no exclamation points.

touchpoints.title: Your space assessment
touchpoints.strategicIntent: An 8-minute assessment that routes the proposal; offers an optional team presence-quiz baseline.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Position the team quiz as optional and self-reported — aggregate only, never individual reporting. The attached call script carries the assessment.

touchpoints.title: Your proposal
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Studio bundle or team program proposal with sizing-day logistics.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Price once, plainly, anchored to what a team reclaims. Sizing day is the logistics spine. One CTA to review the proposal; no expiry pressure.

touchpoints.title: Questions answered
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Handle the B2B objections: pricing, hygiene, sizing at scale, ROI.
touchpoints.contentBrief: One answer per breath. ROI framing uses self-reported presence deltas — never wear data, never surveillance of staff.

touchpoints.title: Decision call
touchpoints.strategicIntent: The human decision call.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Script: confirm fit, name the price once, offer a start date. No closing tricks; the 10-second pause belongs in the call too.

touchpoints.title: Your launch kit
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Sizing-day plan, team pairing guide, and an intro session script for launch.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Checklist tone, one step per line. The intro script includes the 10-second pause — the team's first shared practice.

touchpoints.title: Month one: your team's presence report
touchpoints.strategicIntent: Aggregate, anonymized, self-reported quiz deltas plus a founder check-in.
touchpoints.contentBrief: Aggregate self-reported quiz deltas only — anonymized, opt-in, never individual, never telemetry. Book the month-two review in one tap.
touchpoints.knownRisks: Any individually identifiable data would turn a mindfulness benefit into workplace surveillance.
